examples.title: Recursive Inorder Traversal
examples.description: Python implementation of recursive inorder traversal visiting left-root-right
examples.code:
class TreeNode:
    def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None):
        self.val = val
        self.left = left
        self.right = right

def inorder(root):
    if root is None:
        return []
    result = []
    result.extend(inorder(root.left))    # Left
    result.append(root.val)               # Root
    result.extend(inorder(root.right))   # Right
    return result

# Example usage:
#     15
#    /  \
#  10    25
# Output: [5, 10, 12, 15, 20, 25, 30]
examples.explanation: Recursively visits left subtree, processes current node, then right subtree. For BST produces sorted output.

examples.title: Iterative Level-Order Traversal
examples.description: Java implementation using queue for breadth-first traversal
examples.explanation: Uses FIFO queue. Enqueue root, then process node and enqueue its children. Processes level by level from left to right.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(w) - max width of tree, worst case O(n)

examples.title: Morris Inorder Traversal
examples.description: Constant space inorder traversal using threaded binary tree concept
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MorrisInorder(root)
BEGIN
    current ← root
    result ← []
    
    WHILE current ≠ NULL DO
        IF current.left = NULL THEN
            // No left subtree, visit current
            result.append(current.val)
            current ← current.right
        ELSE
            // Find inorder predecessor
            pre ← current.left
            WHILE pre.right ≠ NULL AND pre.right ≠ current DO
                pre ← pre.right
            END WHILE
            
            // Create temporary thread
            IF pre.right = NULL THEN
                pre.right ← current
                current ← current.left
            ELSE
                // Thread exists, visit current
                result.append(current.val)
                pre.right ← NULL  // Remove thread
                current ← current.right
            END IF
        END IF
    END WHILE
    RETURN result
END
examples.explanation: Morris traversal creates temporary links to inorder predecessor to find path back. No stack or recursion needed. Restores tree after traversal.

examples.title: Recursive Post-Order Traversal
examples.description: C implementation for post-order (Left-Right-Root) traversal used in tree deletion
examples.explanation: Post-order is essential for tree deletion - must free children before parent to prevent memory leaks and dangling pointers.
